channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: 70s-style Robot Anime Geppy-X adds remastered vs. original toggle
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS4
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Xbox Series

Witness the original staff's dedication to detail.

70s-style Robot Anime Geppy-X will include a feature to switch between the game’s newly remastered and original 1999 visuals, publisher Bliss Brain and developer Implicit Conversion announced.

Here are the details:

One of the new features we are most proud to include is the addition of a high-definition remastered version of the animations, digitized directly from the Betacam tapes delivered by the animation studio.

Following the announcement, we received requests from fans who shared that they would love to see the original version of the animations as well. We agree that this is important, especially in the name of preservation, so we have decided to include a feature that allows players to switch between the remastered and original movie versions. This feature will be included in the base game and will not be released as downloadable content.

We invite you to experience the unique atmosphere of the original production, which packed 8,000 hand-drawn animation cels onto four CD-ROMs. Please note that some scenes in the original version were modified by the development team from the Betacam animation, and therefore may differ slightly from the remastered version, which remains faithful to the original Betacam source. We hope you will discover and enjoy the details and creative touches the development team carefully crafted.

70s-style Robot Anime Geppy-X is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PlayStation 4, Switch, and PC via Steam on July 16.

Watch a new trailer below.

New Feature: Switch Between Remastered and Original Movie Versions

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/70s-style-robot-anime-geppy-x-adds-remastered-vs-original-toggle
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round ‘The King of Fighters XIV Collaboration’ trailer
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Mai Shiranui, Kula Diamond, and the newly enhanced "Lost Paradise (Oboro)."

Publisher Koei Tecmo and developer Team NINJA have released a new trailer for DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round introducing the game’s collaboration downloadable content characters Mai Shiranui and Kula Diamond from The King of Fighters XIV, as well as the newly enhanced stage, “Lost Paradise (Oboro).”

DEAD OR ALIVE 6 Last Round is due out for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam on June 25.

Watch the trailer below.

The King of Fighters XIV Collaboration Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/dead-or-alive-6-last-round-the-king-of-fighters-xiv-collaboration-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Stupid Never Dies ‘Styles’ gameplay
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5

Four minutes showcasing the game's 11 transformations.

GPTRACK50 has released a four-minute gameplay video for “blazing fast growth 3D action RPG” Stupid Never Dies introducing the game’s 11 “Styles.”

As previously announced, the game features the following transformations:

  • Zombie Style – The Basic Style for Davy.
  • Werewolf Style – A speedy Style that lets you go on a wild rampage.
  • Harpy Style – A flying Style that attacks distant enemies with projectiles.
  • Golem Style – A tough and sturdy Style that sends packs of enemies flying.
  • Vampire Style – A Style that lets you control a swarm of bats
  • Will-o’-the-Wisp Style – A Style that goes between the physical and astral planes.
  • Cyclops Style – A Style that specializes in big, all-or-nothing attacks.
  • Snow Fairy Style – A Style that freezes enemies and makes their power yours.
  • Merfolk Style – A Style that dives into the ground like water to attack foes.
  • Lich Style – A Style that uses the power of death to command skeletons.
  • Demon Style – A Style that uses whips and gravity to control enemy position.

Stupid Never Dies is due out for PlayStation 5 and PC via Steam in 2026.

Watch the footage below.

Styles Gameplay



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/stupid-never-dies-styles-gameplay
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
